metrics.name: retiring
metrics.desc: Fraction of Top-down slots used by retired uops

metrics.name: fe_bound
metrics.desc: Fraction of slots not delivered by the front end

metrics.name: bad_speculation
metrics.desc: Fraction of slots lost to issued but unretired work and recovery

metrics.name: be_bound
metrics.desc: Fraction of slots blocked on backend resources

metrics.name: be_bound.memory_bound
metrics.desc: Backend slots stalled by outstanding demand memory loads

metrics.name: be_bound.core_bound
metrics.desc: Backend slots not attributed to outstanding demand memory loads

events.name: ARITH.DIVIDER_ACTIVE
events.desc: Counts cycles when divide unit is busy executing divide or square root operations. Accounts for integer and floating-point operations.

events.name: ARITH.FP_DIVIDER_ACTIVE
events.desc: ARITH.FP_DIVIDER_ACTIVE

events.name: ASSISTS.ANY
events.desc: Counts the number of occurrences where a microcode assist is invoked by hardware Examples include AD (page Access Dirty), FP and AVX related assists.

events.name: ASSISTS.FP
events.desc: Counts all microcode Floating Point assists.

events.name: BACLEARS.ANY
events.desc: Counts the number of times the front-end is resteered when it finds a branch instruction in a fetch line. This occurs for the first time a branch instruction is fetched or when the branch is not tracked by the BPU (Branch Prediction Unit) anymore.

events.name: BR_INST_RETIRED.ALL_BRANCHES
events.desc: Counts all branch instructions retired.

events.name: BR_INST_RETIRED.COND
events.desc: Counts conditional branch instructions retired.

events.name: BR_INST_RETIRED.COND_NTAKEN
events.desc: Counts not taken branch instructions retired.

events.name: BR_INST_RETIRED.COND_TAKEN
events.desc: Counts taken conditional branch instructions retired.

events.name: BR_INST_RETIRED.FAR_BRANCH
events.desc: Counts far branch instructions retired.

events.name: BR_INST_RETIRED.INDIRECT
events.desc: Counts near indirect branch instructions retired excluding returns. TSX abort is an indirect branch.

events.name: BR_INST_RETIRED.NEAR_CALL
events.desc: Counts both direct and indirect near call instructions retired.

events.name: BR_INST_RETIRED.NEAR_RETURN
events.desc: Counts return instructions retired.

events.name: BR_INST_RETIRED.NEAR_TAKEN
events.desc: Counts taken branch instructions retired.

events.name: BR_MISP_RETIRED.ALL_BRANCHES
events.desc: Counts all the retired branch instructions that were mispredicted by the processor. A branch misprediction occurs when the processor incorrectly predicts the destination of the branch.  When the misprediction is discovered at execution, all the instructions executed in the wrong (speculative) path must be discarded, and the processor must start fetching from the correct path.

events.name: BR_MISP_RETIRED.COND
events.desc: Counts mispredicted conditional branch instructions retired.

events.name: BR_MISP_RETIRED.COND_NTAKEN
events.desc: Counts the number of conditional branch instructions retired that were mispredicted and the branch direction was not taken.

events.name: BR_MISP_RETIRED.COND_TAKEN
events.desc: Counts taken conditional mispredicted branch instructions retired.

events.name: BR_MISP_RETIRED.INDIRECT
events.desc: Counts all miss-predicted indirect branch instructions retired (excluding RETs. TSX aborts is considered indirect branch).

events.name: BR_MISP_RETIRED.INDIRECT_CALL
events.desc: Counts retired mispredicted indirect (near taken) CALL instructions, including both register and memory indirect.

events.name: BR_MISP_RETIRED.NEAR_TAKEN
events.desc: Counts number of near branch instructions retired that were mispredicted and taken.

events.name: BR_MISP_RETIRED.RET
events.desc: This is a non-precise version (that is, does not use PEBS) of the event that counts mispredicted return instructions retired.

events.name: CORE_POWER.LVL0_TURBO_LICENSE
events.desc: Counts Core cycles where the core was running with power-delivery for baseline license level 0.  This includes non-AVX codes, SSE, AVX 128-bit, and low-current AVX 256-bit codes.

events.name: CORE_POWER.LVL1_TURBO_LICENSE
events.desc: Counts Core cycles where the core was running with power-delivery for license level 1.  This includes high current AVX 256-bit instructions as well as low current AVX 512-bit instructions.

events.name: CORE_POWER.LVL2_TURBO_LICENSE
events.desc: Core cycles where the core was running with power-delivery for license level 2 (introduced in Skylake Server microarchitecture).  This includes high current AVX 512-bit instructions.

events.name: CPU_CLK_UNHALTED.DISTRIBUTED
events.desc: This event distributes cycle counts between active hyperthreads, i.e., those in C0.  A hyperthread becomes inactive when it executes the HLT or MWAIT instructions.  If all other hyperthreads are inactive (or disabled or do not exist), all counts are attributed to this hyperthread. To obtain the full count when the Core is active, sum the counts from each hyperthread.

events.name: CPU_CLK_UNHALTED.ONE_THREAD_ACTIVE
events.desc: Counts Core crystal clock cycles when current thread is unhalted and the other thread is halted.

events.name: CPU_CLK_UNHALTED.REF_DISTRIBUTED
events.desc: This event distributes Core crystal clock cycle counts between active hyperthreads, i.e., those in C0 sleep-state. A hyperthread becomes inactive when it executes the HLT or MWAIT instructions. If one thread is active in a core, all counts are attributed to this hyperthread. To obtain the full count when the Core is active, sum the counts from each hyperthread.

events.name: CPU_CLK_UNHALTED.REF_XCLK
events.desc: Counts core crystal clock cycles when the thread is unhalted.

events.name: CPU_CLK_UNHALTED.THREAD_P
events.desc: This is an architectural event that counts the number of thread cycles while the thread is not in a halt state. The thread enters the halt state when it is running the HLT instruction. The core frequency may change from time to time due to power or thermal throttling. For this reason, this event may have a changing ratio with regards to wall clock time.

events.name: CYCLE_ACTIVITY.CYCLES_L1D_MISS
events.desc: Cycles while L1 cache miss demand load is outstanding.

events.name: CYCLE_ACTIVITY.CYCLES_L2_MISS
events.desc: Cycles while L2 cache miss demand load is outstanding.

events.name: CYCLE_ACTIVITY.CYCLES_MEM_ANY
events.desc: Cycles while memory subsystem has an outstanding load.

events.name: CYCLE_ACTIVITY.STALLS_L1D_MISS
events.desc: Execution stalls while L1 cache miss demand load is outstanding.

events.name: CYCLE_ACTIVITY.STALLS_L2_MISS
events.desc: Execution stalls while L2 cache miss demand load is outstanding.

events.name: CYCLE_ACTIVITY.STALLS_L3_MISS
events.desc: Execution stalls while L3 cache miss demand load is outstanding.

events.name: CYCLE_ACTIVITY.STALLS_MEM_ANY
events.desc: Execution stalls while memory subsystem has an outstanding load.

events.name: CYCLE_ACTIVITY.STALLS_TOTAL
events.desc: Total execution stalls.

events.name: DECODE.LCP
events.desc: Counts cycles that the Instruction Length decoder (ILD) stalls occurred due to dynamically changing prefix length of the decoded instruction (by operand size prefix instruction 0x66, address size prefix instruction 0x67 or REX.W for Intel64). Count is proportional to the number of prefixes in a 16B-line. This may result in a three-cycle penalty for each LCP (Length changing prefix) in a 16-byte chunk. [This event is alias to ILD_STALL.LCP]

events.name: DSB2MITE_SWITCHES.COUNT
events.desc: Counts the number of Decode Stream Buffer (DSB a.k.a. Uop Cache)-to-MITE speculative transitions.

events.name: DSB2MITE_SWITCHES.PENALTY_CYCLES
events.desc: Decode Stream Buffer (DSB) is a Uop-cache that holds translations of previously fetched instructions that were decoded by the legacy x86 decode pipeline (MITE). This event counts fetch penalty cycles when a transition occurs from DSB to MITE.

events.name: DTLB_LOAD_MISSES.STLB_HIT
events.desc: Counts loads that miss the DTLB (Data TLB) and hit the STLB (Second level TLB).

events.name: DTLB_LOAD_MISSES.WALK_ACTIVE
events.desc: Counts cycles when at least one PMH (Page Miss Handler) is busy with a page walk for a demand load.

events.name: DTLB_LOAD_MISSES.WALK_COMPLETED
events.desc: Counts completed page walks  (all page sizes) caused by demand data loads. This implies it missed in the DTLB and further levels of TLB. The page walk can end with or without a fault.

events.name: DTLB_LOAD_MISSES.WALK_COMPLETED_1G
events.desc: Counts completed page walks  (1G sizes) caused by demand data loads. This implies address translations missed in the DTLB and further levels of TLB. The page walk can end with or without a fault.

events.name: DTLB_LOAD_MISSES.WALK_COMPLETED_2M_4M
events.desc: Counts completed page walks  (2M/4M sizes) caused by demand data loads. This implies address translations missed in the DTLB and further levels of TLB. The page walk can end with or without a fault.

events.name: DTLB_LOAD_MISSES.WALK_COMPLETED_4K
events.desc: Counts completed page walks  (4K sizes) caused by demand data loads. This implies address translations missed in the DTLB and further levels of TLB. The page walk can end with or without a fault.

events.name: DTLB_LOAD_MISSES.WALK_PENDING
events.desc: Counts the number of page walks outstanding for a demand load in the PMH (Page Miss Handler) each cycle.

events.name: DTLB_STORE_MISSES.STLB_HIT
events.desc: Counts stores that miss the DTLB (Data TLB) and hit the STLB (2nd Level TLB).

events.name: DTLB_STORE_MISSES.WALK_ACTIVE
events.desc: Counts cycles when at least one PMH (Page Miss Handler) is busy with a page walk for a store.

events.name: DTLB_STORE_MISSES.WALK_COMPLETED
events.desc: Counts completed page walks  (all page sizes) caused by demand data stores. This implies it missed in the DTLB and further levels of TLB. The page walk can end with or without a fault.

events.name: DTLB_STORE_MISSES.WALK_COMPLETED_1G
events.desc: Counts page walks completed due to demand data stores whose address translations missed in the TLB and were mapped to 1G pages.  The page walks can end with or without a page fault.

events.name: DTLB_STORE_MISSES.WALK_COMPLETED_2M_4M
events.desc: Counts page walks completed due to demand data stores whose address translations missed in the TLB and were mapped to 2M/4M pages.  The page walks can end with or without a page fault.

events.name: DTLB_STORE_MISSES.WALK_COMPLETED_4K
events.desc: Counts page walks completed due to demand data stores whose address translations missed in the TLB and were mapped to 4K pages.  The page walks can end with or without a page fault.

events.name: DTLB_STORE_MISSES.WALK_PENDING
events.desc: Counts the number of page walks outstanding for a store in the PMH (Page Miss Handler) each cycle.

events.name: EXE_ACTIVITY.1_PORTS_UTIL
events.desc: Counts cycles during which a total of 1 uop was executed on all ports and Reservation Station (RS) was not empty.

events.name: EXE_ACTIVITY.2_PORTS_UTIL
events.desc: Counts cycles during which a total of 2 uops were executed on all ports and Reservation Station (RS) was not empty.

events.name: EXE_ACTIVITY.3_PORTS_UTIL
events.desc: Cycles total of 3 uops are executed on all ports and Reservation Station (RS) was not empty.

events.name: EXE_ACTIVITY.4_PORTS_UTIL
events.desc: Cycles total of 4 uops are executed on all ports and Reservation Station (RS) was not empty.

events.name: EXE_ACTIVITY.BOUND_ON_LOADS
events.desc: Counts cycles when the memory subsystem has an outstanding load. Increments by 4 for every such cycle.

events.name: EXE_ACTIVITY.BOUND_ON_STORES
events.desc: Counts cycles where the Store Buffer was full and no loads caused an execution stall.

events.name: EXE_ACTIVITY.EXE_BOUND_0_PORTS
events.desc: Number of cycles total of 0 uops executed on all ports, Reservation Station (RS) was not empty, the Store Buffer (SB) was not full and there was no outstanding load.

events.name: FP_ARITH_INST_RETIRED.128B_PACKED_DOUBLE
events.desc: Number of SSE/AVX computational 128-bit packed double precision floating-point instructions retired; some instructions will count twice as noted below.  Each count represents 2 computation operations, one for each element.  Applies to SSE* and AVX* packed double precision floating-point instructions: ADD SUB HADD HSUB SUBADD MUL DIV MIN MAX SQRT DPP FM(N)ADD/SUB.  DPP and FM(N)ADD/SUB instructions count twice as they perform 2 calculations per element. The DAZ and FTZ flags in the MXCSR register need to be set when using these events.

events.name: FP_ARITH_INST_RETIRED.128B_PACKED_SINGLE
events.desc: Number of SSE/AVX computational 128-bit packed single precision floating-point instructions retired; some instructions will count twice as noted below.  Each count represents 4 computation operations, one for each element.  Applies to SSE* and AVX* packed single precision floating-point instructions: ADD SUB HADD HSUB SUBADD MUL DIV MIN MAX SQRT RSQRT RCP DPP FM(N)ADD/SUB.  DPP and FM(N)ADD/SUB instructions count twice as they perform 2 calculations per element. The DAZ and FTZ flags in the MXCSR register need to be set when using these events.

events.name: FP_ARITH_INST_RETIRED.256B_PACKED_DOUBLE
events.desc: Number of SSE/AVX computational 256-bit packed double precision floating-point instructions retired; some instructions will count twice as noted below.  Each count represents 4 computation operations, one for each element.  Applies to SSE* and AVX* packed double precision floating-point instructions: ADD SUB HADD HSUB SUBADD MUL DIV MIN MAX SQRT FM(N)ADD/SUB.  FM(N)ADD/SUB instructions count twice as they perform 2 calculations per element. The DAZ and FTZ flags in the MXCSR register need to be set when using these events.

events.name: FP_ARITH_INST_RETIRED.256B_PACKED_SINGLE
events.desc: Number of SSE/AVX computational 256-bit packed single precision floating-point instructions retired; some instructions will count twice as noted below.  Each count represents 8 computation operations, one for each element.  Applies to SSE* and AVX* packed single precision floating-point instructions: ADD SUB HADD HSUB SUBADD MUL DIV MIN MAX SQRT RSQRT RCP DPP FM(N)ADD/SUB.  DPP and FM(N)ADD/SUB instructions count twice as they perform 2 calculations per element. The DAZ and FTZ flags in the MXCSR register need to be set when using these events.

events.name: FP_ARITH_INST_RETIRED.4_FLOPS
events.desc: Number of SSE/AVX computational 128-bit packed single precision and 256-bit packed double precision  floating-point instructions retired; some instructions will count twice as noted below.  Each count represents 2 or/and 4 computation operations, one for each element.  Applies to SSE* and AVX* packed single precision floating-point and packed double precision floating-point instructions: ADD SUB HADD HSUB SUBADD MUL DIV MIN MAX RCP14 RSQRT14 SQRT DPP FM(N)ADD/SUB.  DPP and FM(N)ADD/SUB instructions count twice as they perform 2 calculations per element. The DAZ and FTZ flags in the MXCSR register need to be set when using these events.

events.name: FP_ARITH_INST_RETIRED.512B_PACKED_DOUBLE
events.desc: Number of SSE/AVX computational 512-bit packed double precision floating-point instructions retired; some instructions will count twice as noted below.  Each count represents 8 computation operations, one for each element.  Applies to SSE* and AVX* packed double precision floating-point instructions: ADD SUB MUL DIV MIN MAX SQRT RSQRT14 RCP14 FM(N)ADD/SUB. FM(N)ADD/SUB instructions count twice as they perform 2 calculations per element. The DAZ and FTZ flags in the MXCSR register need to be set when using these events.

events.name: FP_ARITH_INST_RETIRED.512B_PACKED_SINGLE
events.desc: Number of SSE/AVX computational 512-bit packed single precision floating-point instructions retired; some instructions will count twice as noted below.  Each count represents 16 computation operations, one for each element.  Applies to SSE* and AVX* packed single precision floating-point instructions: ADD SUB MUL DIV MIN MAX SQRT RSQRT14 RCP14 FM(N)ADD/SUB. FM(N)ADD/SUB instructions count twice as they perform 2 calculations per element. The DAZ and FTZ flags in the MXCSR register need to be set when using these events.

events.name: FP_ARITH_INST_RETIRED.8_FLOPS
events.desc: Number of SSE/AVX computational 256-bit packed single precision and 512-bit packed double precision  floating-point instructions retired; some instructions will count twice as noted below.  Each count represents 8 computation operations, one for each element.  Applies to SSE* and AVX* packed single precision and double precision floating-point instructions: ADD SUB HADD HSUB SUBADD MUL DIV MIN MAX SQRT RSQRT RSQRT14 RCP RCP14 DPP FM(N)ADD/SUB.  DPP and FM(N)ADD/SUB instructions count twice as they perform 2 calculations per element. The DAZ and FTZ flags in the MXCSR register need to be set when using these events.

events.name: FP_ARITH_INST_RETIRED.SCALAR
events.desc: Number of SSE/AVX computational scalar single precision and double precision floating-point instructions retired; some instructions will count twice as noted below.  Each count represents 1 computational operation. Applies to SSE* and AVX* scalar single precision floating-point instructions: ADD SUB MUL DIV MIN MAX SQRT RSQRT RCP FM(N)ADD/SUB.  FM(N)ADD/SUB instructions count twice as they perform 2 calculations per element. The DAZ and FTZ flags in the MXCSR register need to be set when using these events.

events.name: FP_ARITH_INST_RETIRED.SCALAR_DOUBLE
events.desc: Number of SSE/AVX computational scalar double precision floating-point instructions retired; some instructions will count twice as noted below.  Each count represents 1 computational operation. Applies to SSE* and AVX* scalar double precision floating-point instructions: ADD SUB MUL DIV MIN MAX SQRT FM(N)ADD/SUB.  FM(N)ADD/SUB instructions count twice as they perform 2 calculations per element. The DAZ and FTZ flags in the MXCSR register need to be set when using these events.

events.name: FP_ARITH_INST_RETIRED.SCALAR_SINGLE
events.desc: Number of SSE/AVX computational scalar single precision floating-point instructions retired; some instructions will count twice as noted below.  Each count represents 1 computational operation. Applies to SSE* and AVX* scalar single precision floating-point instructions: ADD SUB MUL DIV MIN MAX SQRT RSQRT RCP FM(N)ADD/SUB.  FM(N)ADD/SUB instructions count twice as they perform 2 calculations per element. The DAZ and FTZ flags in the MXCSR register need to be set when using these events.

events.name: FP_ARITH_INST_RETIRED.VECTOR
events.desc: Number of any Vector retired FP arithmetic instructions

events.name: ICACHE_16B.IFDATA_STALL
events.desc: Counts cycles where a code line fetch is stalled due to an L1 instruction cache miss. The legacy decode pipeline works at a 16 Byte granularity. [This event is alias to ICACHE_DATA.STALLS]

events.name: ICACHE_64B.IFTAG_HIT
events.desc: Counts instruction fetch tag lookups that hit in the instruction cache (L1I). Counts at 64-byte cache-line granularity. Accounts for both cacheable and uncacheable accesses.

events.name: ICACHE_64B.IFTAG_MISS
events.desc: Counts instruction fetch tag lookups that miss in the instruction cache (L1I). Counts at 64-byte cache-line granularity. Accounts for both cacheable and uncacheable accesses.

events.name: ICACHE_64B.IFTAG_STALL
events.desc: Counts cycles where a code fetch is stalled due to L1 instruction cache tag miss. [This event is alias to ICACHE_TAG.STALLS]

events.name: ICACHE_DATA.STALLS
events.desc: Counts cycles where a code line fetch is stalled due to an L1 instruction cache miss. The legacy decode pipeline works at a 16 Byte granularity. [This event is alias to ICACHE_16B.IFDATA_STALL]

events.name: ICACHE_TAG.STALLS
events.desc: Counts cycles where a code fetch is stalled due to L1 instruction cache tag miss. [This event is alias to ICACHE_64B.IFTAG_STALL]

events.name: IDQ.DSB_CYCLES_ANY
events.desc: Counts the number of cycles uops were delivered to Instruction Decode Queue (IDQ) from the Decode Stream Buffer (DSB) path.

events.name: IDQ.DSB_CYCLES_OK
events.desc: Counts the number of cycles where optimal number of uops was delivered to the Instruction Decode Queue (IDQ) from the DSB (Decode Stream Buffer) path. Count includes uops that may 'bypass' the IDQ.

events.name: IDQ.DSB_UOPS
events.desc: Counts the number of uops delivered to Instruction Decode Queue (IDQ) from the Decode Stream Buffer (DSB) path.

events.name: IDQ.MITE_CYCLES_ANY
events.desc: Counts the number of cycles uops were delivered to the Instruction Decode Queue (IDQ) from the MITE (legacy decode pipeline) path. During these cycles uops are not being delivered from the Decode Stream Buffer (DSB).

events.name: IDQ.MITE_CYCLES_OK
events.desc: Counts the number of cycles where optimal number of uops was delivered to the Instruction Decode Queue (IDQ) from the MITE (legacy decode pipeline) path. During these cycles uops are not being delivered from the Decode Stream Buffer (DSB).

events.name: IDQ.MITE_UOPS
events.desc: Counts the number of uops delivered to Instruction Decode Queue (IDQ) from the MITE path. This also means that uops are not being delivered from the Decode Stream Buffer (DSB).

events.name: IDQ.MS_CYCLES_ANY
events.desc: Counts cycles during which uops are being delivered to Instruction Decode Queue (IDQ) while the Microcode Sequencer (MS) is busy. Uops maybe initiated by Decode Stream Buffer (DSB) or MITE.

events.name: IDQ.MS_SWITCHES
events.desc: Number of switches from DSB (Decode Stream Buffer) or MITE (legacy decode pipeline) to the Microcode Sequencer.

events.name: IDQ.MS_UOPS
events.desc: Counts the total number of uops delivered by the Microcode Sequencer (MS). Any instruction over 4 uops will be delivered by the MS. Some instructions such as transcendentals may additionally generate uops from the MS.

events.name: IDQ_UOPS_NOT_DELIVERED.CORE
events.desc: Counts the number of uops not delivered to by the Instruction Decode Queue (IDQ) to the back-end of the pipeline when there was no back-end stalls. This event counts for one SMT thread in a given cycle.

events.name: IDQ_UOPS_NOT_DELIVERED.CYCLES_0_UOPS_DELIV.CORE
events.desc: Counts the number of cycles when no uops were delivered by the Instruction Decode Queue (IDQ) to the back-end of the pipeline when there was no back-end stalls. This event counts for one SMT thread in a given cycle.

events.name: IDQ_UOPS_NOT_DELIVERED.CYCLES_FE_WAS_OK
events.desc: Counts the number of cycles when the optimal number of uops were delivered by the Instruction Decode Queue (IDQ) to the back-end of the pipeline when there was no back-end stalls. This event counts for one SMT thread in a given cycle.

events.name: ILD_STALL.LCP
events.desc: Counts cycles that the Instruction Length decoder (ILD) stalls occurred due to dynamically changing prefix length of the decoded instruction (by operand size prefix instruction 0x66, address size prefix instruction 0x67 or REX.W for Intel64). Count is proportional to the number of prefixes in a 16B-line. This may result in a three-cycle penalty for each LCP (Length changing prefix) in a 16-byte chunk. [This event is alias to DECODE.LCP]

events.name: INST_DECODED.DECODERS
events.desc: Number of decoders utilized in a cycle when the MITE (legacy decode pipeline) fetches instructions.

events.name: INST_RETIRED.ANY_P
events.desc: Counts the number of X86 instructions retired - an Architectural PerfMon event. Counting continues during hardware interrupts, traps, and inside interrupt handlers. Notes: INST_RETIRED.ANY is counted by a designated fixed counter freeing up programmable counters to count other events. INST_RETIRED.ANY_P is counted by a programmable counter.

events.name: INST_RETIRED.NOP
events.desc: Counts all retired NOP or ENDBR32/64 instructions

events.name: INT_MISC.ALL_RECOVERY_CYCLES
events.desc: Counts cycles the Backend cluster is recovering after a miss-speculation or a Store Buffer or Load Buffer drain stall.

events.name: INT_MISC.CLEARS_COUNT
events.desc: Counts the number of speculative clears due to any type of branch misprediction or machine clears

events.name: INT_MISC.CLEAR_RESTEER_CYCLES
events.desc: Cycles after recovery from a branch misprediction or machine clear till the first uop is issued from the resteered path.

events.name: INT_MISC.RECOVERY_CYCLES
events.desc: Counts core cycles when the Resource allocator was stalled due to recovery from an earlier branch misprediction or machine clear event.

events.name: INT_MISC.UOP_DROPPING
events.desc: Estimated number of Top-down Microarchitecture Analysis slots that got dropped due to non front-end reasons

events.name: ITLB_MISSES.STLB_HIT
events.desc: Counts instruction fetch requests that miss the ITLB (Instruction TLB) and hit the STLB (Second-level TLB).

events.name: ITLB_MISSES.WALK_ACTIVE
events.desc: Counts cycles when at least one PMH (Page Miss Handler) is busy with a page walk for a code (instruction fetch) request.

events.name: ITLB_MISSES.WALK_COMPLETED
events.desc: Counts completed page walks (all page sizes) caused by a code fetch. This implies it missed in the ITLB (Instruction TLB) and further levels of TLB. The page walk can end with or without a fault.

events.name: ITLB_MISSES.WALK_COMPLETED_2M_4M
events.desc: Counts completed page walks (2M/4M page sizes) caused by a code fetch. This implies it missed in the ITLB (Instruction TLB) and further levels of TLB. The page walk can end with or without a fault.

events.name: ITLB_MISSES.WALK_COMPLETED_4K
events.desc: Counts completed page walks (4K page sizes) caused by a code fetch. This implies it missed in the ITLB (Instruction TLB) and further levels of TLB. The page walk can end with or without a fault.

events.name: ITLB_MISSES.WALK_PENDING
events.desc: Counts the number of page walks outstanding for an outstanding code (instruction fetch) request in the PMH (Page Miss Handler) each cycle.

events.name: L1D.REPLACEMENT
events.desc: Counts L1D data line replacements including opportunistic replacements, and replacements that require stall-for-replace or block-for-replace.

events.name: L1D_PEND_MISS.FB_FULL
events.desc: Counts number of cycles a demand request has waited due to L1D Fill Buffer (FB) unavailability. Demand requests include cacheable/uncacheable demand load, store, lock or SW prefetch accesses.

events.name: L1D_PEND_MISS.FB_FULL_PERIODS
events.desc: Counts number of phases a demand request has waited due to L1D Fill Buffer (FB) unavailability. Demand requests include cacheable/uncacheable demand load, store, lock or SW prefetch accesses.

events.name: L1D_PEND_MISS.L2_STALL
events.desc: Counts number of cycles a demand request has waited due to L1D due to lack of L2 resources. Demand requests include cacheable/uncacheable demand load, store, lock or SW prefetch accesses.

events.name: L1D_PEND_MISS.PENDING
events.desc: Counts number of L1D misses that are outstanding in each cycle, that is each cycle the number of Fill Buffers (FB) outstanding required by Demand Reads. FB either is held by demand loads, or it is held by non-demand loads and gets hit at least once by demand. The valid outstanding interval is defined until the FB deallocation by one of the following ways: from FB allocation, if FB is allocated by demand from the demand Hit FB, if it is allocated by hardware or software prefetch. Note: In the L1D, a Demand Read contains cacheable or noncacheable demand loads, including ones causing cache-line splits and reads due to page walks resulted from any request type.

events.name: L1D_PEND_MISS.PENDING_CYCLES
events.desc: Counts duration of L1D miss outstanding in cycles.

events.name: L2_LINES_IN.ALL
events.desc: Counts the number of L2 cache lines filling the L2. Counting does not cover rejects.

events.name: L2_LINES_OUT.NON_SILENT
events.desc: Counts the number of lines that are evicted by L2 cache when triggered by an L2 cache fill. Those lines are in Modified state. Modified lines are written back to L3

events.name: L2_LINES_OUT.SILENT
events.desc: Counts the number of lines that are silently dropped by L2 cache. These lines are typically in Shared or Exclusive state. A non-threaded event.

events.name: L2_LINES_OUT.USELESS_HWPF
events.desc: Counts the number of cache lines that have been prefetched by the L2 hardware prefetcher but not used by demand access when evicted from the L2 cache

events.name: L2_RQSTS.ALL_CODE_RD
events.desc: Counts the total number of L2 code requests.

events.name: L2_RQSTS.ALL_DEMAND_DATA_RD
events.desc: Counts Demand Data Read requests accessing the L2 cache. These requests may hit or miss L2 cache. True-miss exclude misses that were merged with ongoing L2 misses. An access is counted once.

events.name: L2_RQSTS.ALL_RFO
events.desc: Counts the total number of RFO (read for ownership) requests to L2 cache. L2 RFO requests include both L1D demand RFO misses as well as L1D RFO prefetches.

events.name: L2_RQSTS.CODE_RD_HIT
events.desc: Counts L2 cache hits when fetching instructions, code reads.

events.name: L2_RQSTS.CODE_RD_MISS
events.desc: Counts L2 cache misses when fetching instructions.

events.name: L2_RQSTS.DEMAND_DATA_RD_HIT
events.desc: Counts the number of demand Data Read requests initiated by load instructions that hit L2 cache.

events.name: L2_RQSTS.DEMAND_DATA_RD_MISS
events.desc: Counts demand Data Read requests with true-miss in the L2 cache. True-miss excludes misses that were merged with ongoing L2 misses. An access is counted once.

events.name: L2_RQSTS.MISS
events.desc: Counts read requests of any type with true-miss in the L2 cache. True-miss excludes L2 misses that were merged with ongoing L2 misses.

events.name: L2_RQSTS.REFERENCES
events.desc: Counts all requests that were hit or true misses in L2 cache. True-miss excludes misses that were merged with ongoing L2 misses.

events.name: L2_RQSTS.RFO_HIT
events.desc: Counts the RFO (Read-for-Ownership) requests that hit L2 cache.

events.name: L2_RQSTS.RFO_MISS
events.desc: Counts the RFO (Read-for-Ownership) requests that miss L2 cache.

events.name: L2_RQSTS.SWPF_HIT
events.desc: Counts Software prefetch requests that hit the L2 cache. Accounts for PREFETCHNTA and PREFETCHT0/1/2 instructions when FB is not full.

events.name: L2_RQSTS.SWPF_MISS
events.desc: Counts Software prefetch requests that miss the L2 cache. Accounts for PREFETCHNTA and PREFETCHT0/1/2 instructions when FB is not full.

events.name: L2_TRANS.L2_WB
events.desc: Counts L2 writebacks that access L2 cache.

events.name: LD_BLOCKS.NO_SR
events.desc: Counts the number of times that split load operations are temporarily blocked because all resources for handling the split accesses are in use.

events.name: LD_BLOCKS.STORE_FORWARD
events.desc: Counts the number of times where store forwarding was prevented for a load operation. The most common case is a load blocked due to the address of memory access (partially) overlapping with a preceding uncompleted store. Note: See the table of not supported store forwards in the Optimization Guide.

events.name: LD_BLOCKS_PARTIAL.ADDRESS_ALIAS
events.desc: Counts the number of times a load got blocked due to false dependencies in MOB due to partial compare on address.

events.name: LOAD_HIT_PREFETCH.SWPF
events.desc: Counts all software-prefetch load dispatches that hit the fill buffer (FB) allocated for the software prefetch. It can also be incremented by some lock instructions. So it should only be used with profiling so that the locks can be excluded by ASM (Assembly File) inspection of the nearby instructions.

events.name: LOCK_CYCLES.CACHE_LOCK_DURATION
events.desc: This event counts the number of cycles when the L1D is locked. It is a superset of the 0x1 mask (BUS_LOCK_CLOCKS.BUS_LOCK_DURATION).

events.name: LONGEST_LAT_CACHE.MISS
events.desc: Counts core-originated cacheable requests that miss the L3 cache (Longest Latency cache). Requests include data and code reads, Reads-for-Ownership (RFOs), speculative accesses and hardware prefetches to the L1 and L2.  It does not include hardware prefetches to the L3, and may not count other types of requests to the L3.

events.name: LSD.CYCLES_ACTIVE
events.desc: Counts the cycles when at least one uop is delivered by the LSD (Loop-stream detector).

events.name: LSD.CYCLES_OK
events.desc: Counts the cycles when optimal number of uops is delivered by the LSD (Loop-stream detector).

events.name: LSD.UOPS
events.desc: Counts the number of uops delivered to the back-end by the LSD(Loop Stream Detector).

events.name: MACHINE_CLEARS.COUNT
events.desc: Counts the number of machine clears (nukes) of any type.

events.name: MACHINE_CLEARS.MEMORY_ORDERING
events.desc: Counts the number of Machine Clears detected dye to memory ordering. Memory Ordering Machine Clears may apply when a memory read may not conform to the memory ordering rules of the x86 architecture

events.name: MACHINE_CLEARS.SMC
events.desc: Counts self-modifying code (SMC) detected, which causes a machine clear.

events.name: MEM_INST_RETIRED.ALL_LOADS
events.desc: Counts all retired load instructions. This event accounts for SW prefetch instructions of PREFETCHNTA or PREFETCHT0/1/2 or PREFETCHW.

events.name: MEM_INST_RETIRED.ALL_STORES
events.desc: Counts all retired store instructions.

events.name: MEM_INST_RETIRED.ANY
events.desc: Counts all retired memory instructions - loads and stores.

events.name: MEM_INST_RETIRED.LOCK_LOADS
events.desc: Counts retired load instructions with locked access.

events.name: MEM_INST_RETIRED.SPLIT_LOADS
events.desc: Counts retired load instructions that split across a cacheline boundary.

events.name: MEM_INST_RETIRED.SPLIT_STORES
events.desc: Counts retired store instructions that split across a cacheline boundary.

events.name: MEM_INST_RETIRED.STLB_MISS_LOADS
events.desc: Number of retired load instructions that (start a) miss in the 2nd-level TLB (STLB).

events.name: MEM_INST_RETIRED.STLB_MISS_STORES
events.desc: Number of retired store instructions that (start a) miss in the 2nd-level TLB (STLB).

events.name: MEM_LOAD_L3_HIT_RETIRED.XSNP_FWD
events.desc: Counts retired load instructions where a cross-core snoop hit in another cores caches on this socket, the data was forwarded back to the requesting core as the data was modified (SNOOP_HITM) or the L3 did not have the data(SNOOP_HIT_WITH_FWD).

events.name: MEM_LOAD_L3_HIT_RETIRED.XSNP_MISS
events.desc: Counts the retired load instructions whose data sources were L3 hit and cross-core snoop missed in on-pkg core cache.

events.name: MEM_LOAD_L3_HIT_RETIRED.XSNP_NONE
events.desc: Counts retired load instructions whose data sources were hits in L3 without snoops required.

events.name: MEM_LOAD_L3_HIT_RETIRED.XSNP_NO_FWD
events.desc: Counts retired load instructions in which the L3 supplied the data and a cross-core snoop hit in another cores caches on this socket but that other core did not forward the data back (SNOOP_HIT_NO_FWD).

events.name: MEM_LOAD_MISC_RETIRED.UC
events.desc: Retired instructions with at least one load to uncacheable memory-type, or at least one cache-line split locked access

events.name: MEM_LOAD_RETIRED.FB_HIT
events.desc: Counts retired load instructions with at least one uop was load missed in L1 but hit FB (Fill Buffers) due to preceding miss to the same cache line with data not ready.

events.name: MEM_LOAD_RETIRED.L1_HIT
events.desc: Counts retired load instructions with at least one uop that hit in the L1 data cache. This event includes all SW prefetches and lock instructions regardless of the data source.

events.name: MEM_LOAD_RETIRED.L1_MISS
events.desc: Counts retired load instructions with at least one uop that missed in the L1 cache.

events.name: MEM_LOAD_RETIRED.L2_HIT
events.desc: Counts retired load instructions with L2 cache hits as data sources.

events.name: MEM_LOAD_RETIRED.L2_MISS
events.desc: Counts retired load instructions missed L2 cache as data sources.

events.name: MEM_LOAD_RETIRED.L3_HIT
events.desc: Counts retired load instructions with at least one uop that hit in the L3 cache.

events.name: MEM_LOAD_RETIRED.L3_MISS
events.desc: Counts retired load instructions with at least one uop that missed in the L3 cache.

events.name: MISC_RETIRED.LBR_INSERTS
events.desc: Increments when an entry is added to the Last Branch Record (LBR) array (or removed from the array in case of RETURNs in call stack mode). The event requires LBR enable via IA32_DEBUGCTL MSR and branch type selection via MSR_LBR_SELECT.

events.name: MISC_RETIRED.PAUSE_INST
events.desc: Counts number of retired PAUSE instructions. This event is not supported on first SKL and KBL products.

events.name: OFFCORE_REQUESTS.ALL_DATA_RD
events.desc: Counts the demand and prefetch data reads. All Core Data Reads include cacheable 'Demands' and L2 prefetchers (not L3 prefetchers). Counting also covers reads due to page walks resulted from any request type.

events.name: OFFCORE_REQUESTS.ALL_REQUESTS
events.desc: Counts memory transactions reached the super queue including requests initiated by the core, all L3 prefetches, page walks, etc..

events.name: OFFCORE_REQUESTS.DEMAND_DATA_RD
events.desc: Counts the Demand Data Read requests sent to uncore. Use it in conjunction with OFFCORE_REQUESTS_OUTSTANDING to determine average latency in the uncore.

events.name: OFFCORE_REQUESTS.DEMAND_RFO
events.desc: Counts the demand RFO (read for ownership) requests including regular RFOs, locks, ItoM.

events.name: OFFCORE_REQUESTS.L3_MISS_DEMAND_DATA_RD
events.desc: Demand Data Read requests who miss L3 cache.

events.name: OFFCORE_REQUESTS_OUTSTANDING.ALL_DATA_RD
events.desc: Counts the number of offcore outstanding cacheable Core Data Read transactions in the super queue every cycle. A transaction is considered to be in the Offcore outstanding state between L2 miss and transaction completion sent to requestor (SQ de-allocation). See corresponding Umask under OFFCORE_REQUESTS.

events.name: OFFCORE_REQUESTS_OUTSTANDING.CYCLES_WITH_DATA_RD
events.desc: Counts cycles when offcore outstanding cacheable Core Data Read transactions are present in the super queue. A transaction is considered to be in the Offcore outstanding state between L2 miss and transaction completion sent to requestor (SQ de-allocation). See corresponding Umask under OFFCORE_REQUESTS.

events.name: OFFCORE_REQUESTS_OUTSTANDING.CYCLES_WITH_DEMAND_CODE_RD
events.desc: Counts the number of offcore outstanding Code Reads transactions in the super queue every cycle. The 'Offcore outstanding' state of the transaction lasts from the L2 miss until the sending transaction completion to requestor (SQ deallocation). See the corresponding Umask under OFFCORE_REQUESTS.

events.name: OFFCORE_REQUESTS_OUTSTANDING.CYCLES_WITH_DEMAND_DATA_RD
events.desc: Counts cycles when offcore outstanding Demand Data Read transactions are present in the super queue (SQ). A transaction is considered to be in the Offcore outstanding state between L2 miss and transaction completion sent to requestor (SQ de-allocation).

events.name: OFFCORE_REQUESTS_OUTSTANDING.CYCLES_WITH_DEMAND_RFO
events.desc: Counts the number of offcore outstanding demand rfo Reads transactions in the super queue every cycle. The 'Offcore outstanding' state of the transaction lasts from the L2 miss until the sending transaction completion to requestor (SQ deallocation). See the corresponding Umask under OFFCORE_REQUESTS.

events.name: OFFCORE_REQUESTS_OUTSTANDING.DEMAND_DATA_RD
events.desc: Counts the number of off-core outstanding Demand Data Read transactions every cycle. A transaction is considered to be in the Off-core outstanding state between L2 cache miss and data-return to the core.

events.name: OFFCORE_REQUESTS_OUTSTANDING.DEMAND_DATA_RD_GE_6
events.desc: Cycles with at least 6 offcore outstanding Demand Data Read transactions in uncore queue.

events.name: OFFCORE_REQUESTS_OUTSTANDING.DEMAND_RFO
events.desc: Counts the number of off-core outstanding read-for-ownership (RFO) store transactions every cycle. An RFO transaction is considered to be in the Off-core outstanding state between L2 cache miss and transaction completion.

events.name: RESOURCE_STALLS.SB
events.desc: Counts allocation stall cycles caused by the store buffer (SB) being full. This counts cycles that the pipeline back-end blocked uop delivery from the front-end.

events.name: RESOURCE_STALLS.SCOREBOARD
events.desc: Counts cycles where the pipeline is stalled due to serializing operations.

events.name: RS_EVENTS.EMPTY_CYCLES
events.desc: Counts cycles during which the reservation station (RS) is empty for this logical processor. This is usually caused when the front-end pipeline runs into starvation periods (e.g. branch mispredictions or i-cache misses)

events.name: RS_EVENTS.EMPTY_END
events.desc: Counts end of periods where the Reservation Station (RS) was empty. Could be useful to closely sample on front-end latency issues (see the FRONTEND_RETIRED event of designated precise events)

events.name: RTM_RETIRED.ABORTED
events.desc: Counts the number of times RTM abort was triggered.

events.name: RTM_RETIRED.ABORTED_EVENTS
events.desc: Counts the number of times an RTM execution aborted due to none of the previous 3 categories (e.g. interrupt).

events.name: RTM_RETIRED.ABORTED_MEM
events.desc: Counts the number of times an RTM execution aborted due to various memory events (e.g. read/write capacity and conflicts).

events.name: RTM_RETIRED.ABORTED_MEMTYPE
events.desc: Counts the number of times an RTM execution aborted due to incompatible memory type.

events.name: RTM_RETIRED.ABORTED_UNFRIENDLY
events.desc: Counts the number of times an RTM execution aborted due to HLE-unfriendly instructions.

events.name: RTM_RETIRED.COMMIT
events.desc: Counts the number of times RTM commit succeeded.

events.name: RTM_RETIRED.START
events.desc: Counts the number of times we entered an RTM region. Does not count nested transactions.

events.name: SQ_MISC.BUS_LOCK
events.desc: Counts the more expensive bus lock needed to enforce cache coherency for certain memory accesses that need to be done atomically.  Can be created by issuing an atomic instruction (via the LOCK prefix) which causes a cache line split or accesses uncacheable memory.

events.name: SQ_MISC.SQ_FULL
events.desc: Counts the cycles for which the thread is active and the superQ cannot take any more entries.

events.name: SW_PREFETCH_ACCESS.ANY
events.desc: Counts the number of PREFETCHNTA, PREFETCHW, PREFETCHT0, PREFETCHT1 or PREFETCHT2 instructions executed.

events.name: SW_PREFETCH_ACCESS.NTA
events.desc: Counts the number of PREFETCHNTA instructions executed.

events.name: SW_PREFETCH_ACCESS.PREFETCHW
events.desc: Counts the number of PREFETCHW instructions executed.

events.name: SW_PREFETCH_ACCESS.T0
events.desc: Counts the number of PREFETCHT0 instructions executed.

events.name: SW_PREFETCH_ACCESS.T1_T2
events.desc: Counts the number of PREFETCHT1 or PREFETCHT2 instructions executed.

events.name: TLB_FLUSH.DTLB_THREAD
events.desc: Counts the number of DTLB flush attempts of the thread-specific entries.

events.name: TLB_FLUSH.STLB_ANY
events.desc: Counts the number of any STLB flush attempts (such as entire, VPID, PCID, InvPage, CR3 write, etc.).

events.name: TOPDOWN.BACKEND_BOUND_SLOTS
events.desc: Counts the number of Top-down Microarchitecture Analysis (TMA) method's  slots where no micro-operations were being issued from front-end to back-end of the machine due to lack of back-end resources.

events.name: TOPDOWN.SLOTS_P
events.desc: Counts the number of available slots for an unhalted logical processor. The event increments by machine-width of the narrowest pipeline as employed by the Top-down Microarchitecture Analysis method. The count is distributed among unhalted logical processors (hyper-threads) who share the same physical core.

events.name: TX_EXEC.MISC2
events.desc: Counts Unfriendly TSX abort triggered by a vzeroupper instruction.

events.name: TX_EXEC.MISC3
events.desc: Counts Unfriendly TSX abort triggered by a nest count that is too deep.

events.name: TX_MEM.ABORT_CAPACITY_READ
events.desc: Speculatively counts the number of Transactional Synchronization Extensions (TSX) aborts due to a data capacity limitation for transactional reads

events.name: TX_MEM.ABORT_CAPACITY_WRITE
events.desc: Speculatively counts the number of Transactional Synchronization Extensions (TSX) aborts due to a data capacity limitation for transactional writes.

events.name: TX_MEM.ABORT_CONFLICT
events.desc: Counts the number of times a TSX line had a cache conflict.

events.name: UOPS_DECODED.DEC0
events.desc: Uops exclusively fetched by decoder 0

events.name: UOPS_DISPATCHED.PORT_0
events.desc: Counts, on the per-thread basis, cycles during which at least one uop is dispatched from the Reservation Station (RS) to port 0.

events.name: UOPS_DISPATCHED.PORT_1
events.desc: Counts, on the per-thread basis, cycles during which at least one uop is dispatched from the Reservation Station (RS) to port 1.

events.name: UOPS_DISPATCHED.PORT_2_3
events.desc: Counts, on the per-thread basis, cycles during which at least one uop is dispatched from the Reservation Station (RS) to ports 2 and 3.

events.name: UOPS_DISPATCHED.PORT_4_9
events.desc: Counts, on the per-thread basis, cycles during which at least one uop is dispatched from the Reservation Station (RS) to ports 5 and 9.

events.name: UOPS_DISPATCHED.PORT_5
events.desc: Counts, on the per-thread basis, cycles during which at least one uop is dispatched from the Reservation Station (RS) to port 5.

events.name: UOPS_DISPATCHED.PORT_6
events.desc: Counts, on the per-thread basis, cycles during which at least one uop is dispatched from the Reservation Station (RS) to port 6.

events.name: UOPS_DISPATCHED.PORT_7_8
events.desc: Counts, on the per-thread basis, cycles during which at least one uop is dispatched from the Reservation Station (RS) to ports 7 and 8.

events.name: UOPS_EXECUTED.CORE
events.desc: Counts the number of uops executed from any thread.

events.name: UOPS_EXECUTED.CORE_CYCLES_GE_1
events.desc: Counts cycles when at least 1 micro-op is executed from any thread on physical core.

events.name: UOPS_EXECUTED.CORE_CYCLES_GE_2
events.desc: Counts cycles when at least 2 micro-ops are executed from any thread on physical core.

events.name: UOPS_EXECUTED.CORE_CYCLES_GE_3
events.desc: Counts cycles when at least 3 micro-ops are executed from any thread on physical core.

events.name: UOPS_EXECUTED.CORE_CYCLES_GE_4
events.desc: Counts cycles when at least 4 micro-ops are executed from any thread on physical core.

events.name: UOPS_EXECUTED.CYCLES_GE_1
events.desc: Cycles where at least 1 uop was executed per-thread.

events.name: UOPS_EXECUTED.CYCLES_GE_2
events.desc: Cycles where at least 2 uops were executed per-thread.

events.name: UOPS_EXECUTED.CYCLES_GE_3
events.desc: Cycles where at least 3 uops were executed per-thread.

events.name: UOPS_EXECUTED.CYCLES_GE_4
events.desc: Cycles where at least 4 uops were executed per-thread.

events.name: UOPS_EXECUTED.STALL_CYCLES
events.desc: Counts cycles during which no uops were dispatched from the Reservation Station (RS) per thread.

events.name: UOPS_EXECUTED.THREAD
events.desc: Counts the number of uops to be executed per-thread each cycle.

events.name: UOPS_EXECUTED.X87
events.desc: Counts the number of x87 uops executed.

events.name: UOPS_ISSUED.ANY
events.desc: Counts the number of uops that the Resource Allocation Table (RAT) issues to the Reservation Station (RS).

events.name: UOPS_ISSUED.STALL_CYCLES
events.desc: Counts cycles during which the Resource Allocation Table (RAT) does not issue any Uops to the reservation station (RS) for the current thread.

events.name: UOPS_ISSUED.VECTOR_WIDTH_MISMATCH
events.desc: Counts the number of Blend Uops issued by the Resource Allocation Table (RAT) to the reservation station (RS) in order to preserve upper bits of vector registers. Starting with the Skylake microarchitecture, these Blend uops are needed since every Intel SSE instruction executed in Dirty Upper State needs to preserve bits 128-255 of the destination register. For more information, refer to Mixing Intel AVX and Intel SSE Code section of the Optimization Guide.

events.name: UOPS_RETIRED.SLOTS
events.desc: Counts the retirement slots used each cycle.

events.name: UOPS_RETIRED.STALL_CYCLES
events.desc: This event counts cycles without actually retired uops.

events.name: UOPS_RETIRED.TOTAL_CYCLES
events.desc: Counts the number of cycles using always true condition (uops_ret < 16) applied to non PEBS uops retired event.
